name: Shizuoka Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the seas and skies of Shizuoka and a peaceful future
the sun, citrus fruits and the warm climate of the prefecture
the snow of Mount Fuji and purity

description: A blue field charged at the centre with an orange emblem outlined in white, stylising Mount Fuji and the hiragana し of the prefecture's name.
history: Shizuoka's prefectural symbol was adopted on 12 December 1968. The flag, with the orange-and-white emblem on a blue field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: the deep blue sea and skies of the Pacific coast and Suruga Bay
a stylised silhouette of Mount Fuji, the iconic peak straddling the prefecture, fimbriated white; the form also reads as the hiragana し (shi) of 静岡 (Shizuoka).
